title: Dieteria shastensis
intro: Dieteria shastensis, also known as Shasta dieteria, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the western United States and parts of Canada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Dieteria shastensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its health benefits. In herbal medicine, Dieteria shastensis is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create a topical cream or salve for skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Dieteria shastensis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is native to North America, specifically the western parts of the continent. Dieteria shastensis is a member of the tribe Cichorieae within the family Asteraceae. The plant is further classified into the genus Dieteria, which is part of the larger family Asteraceae. Within the genus Dieteria, Dieteria shastensis is a specific species.
